articles.title: Prime Brokers Race to Give Wall Street Access to Event Bets
articles.summary: Prime brokers are scrambling to offer their Wall Street clients access to prediction markets and event-driven betting platforms. This institutional rush validates prediction markets as a legitimate asset class and could pump serious liquidity into political, economic, and cultural event betting.

articles.title: The Players Championship: Preview plus Kalshi Prediction Markets and Props at TPC Sawgrass
articles.summary: The Players Championship kicks off at TPC Sawgrass this week, with Kalshi offering prediction markets alongside traditional golf props for the tournament. Golf betting has exploded since legalization, and The Players draws massive handle as the unofficial "fifth major" with its iconic island green and $25M purse attracting both casual fans and sharp money.

articles.title: Blumenthal Steps Up Lawmaker Opposition to Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Senator Richard Blumenthal is leading a fresh push by lawmakers to crack down on prediction markets, likely targeting platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket that have gained mainstream attention. This signals regulators are getting serious about reining in political betting just as these markets are proving their value during election cycles, potentially threatening the industry's growth trajectory.

articles.title: Kalshi Still Needs Sports, Even as Courts Close In
articles.summary: Kalshi faces mounting legal pressure but continues pushing for sports betting approval, viewing it as essential to their business model despite regulatory headwinds. Sports markets would give Kalshi the volume and mainstream appeal needed to compete with offshore platforms, making this fight existential rather than opportunistic.

articles.title: Are Prediction Markets Actually Good for Journalism?
articles.summary: Journalists and media critics are debating whether prediction markets help or hurt news reporting, as betting platforms increasingly influence how stories get covered and what gets attention. The outcome could determine whether newsrooms embrace prediction markets as reporting tools or dismiss them as gambling distractions that skew editorial priorities toward whatever drives the most action.

articles.title: The Biggest Bet in Tech Isn't on Polymarket. It's This AI Stock.
articles.summary: A tech stock has become such a massive speculative play that it dwarfs even the wildest action on Polymarket. When traditional equity markets start acting like prediction markets with binary outcomes, it signals that institutional money is placing bets as extreme as any crypto trader.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Fight Is for Next Generation of Financial Markets
articles.summary: Polymarket is battling regulators and traditional finance incumbents to establish prediction markets as a legitimate asset class for mainstream investors. If they win, we're looking at billions in institutional capital flowing into markets that currently operate on the fringes with retail crypto money.

articles.title: Koch Q&A on KY Fixed-Odds Bill: Predictive Markets 'Absolutely Cannibalizing' Other Gambling
articles.summary: A Koch Industries executive told Kentucky lawmakers that prediction markets are eating into traditional gambling revenues as the state considers legalizing fixed-odds sports betting. The admission signals that prediction markets have grown large enough to threaten established gambling interests, which could either accelerate regulatory crackdowns or legitimize the space as serious competition.

articles.title: Is Kalshi vs Polymarket the next OpenAI vs. Anthropic? What to know about the newest fintech rivalry
articles.summary: Two US-based prediction market platforms are squaring off in what could become tech's next major corporate rivalry, with Kalshi and Polymarket competing for dominance in the rapidly growing political betting space. The winner will likely shape how Americans bet on elections and news events for years to come, potentially determining whether prediction markets become mainstream or remain niche.

articles.title: Polymarket Taps Palantir, TWG AI to Police Growing Sports Bets
articles.summary: Polymarket signed deals with Palantir and TWG AI to monitor and detect suspicious activity across its expanding sports betting markets. The move signals serious investment in fraud prevention as sports betting drives massive volume growth, but also suggests regulatory pressure is forcing prediction markets to adopt traditional sportsbook-style surveillance infrastructure.

articles.title: This 29‑Year‑Old Founder Took On a Federal Regulator to Scale Her Company—and Built an $11 Billion Giant
articles.summary: A 29-year-old founder battled federal regulators to grow her startup into an $11 billion company, proving that regulatory friction doesn't always kill ambitious businesses. Her playbook for navigating compliance while scaling fast offers a template that prediction market platforms desperately need as they face their own regulatory gauntlet.

articles.title: Gamblers can now bet on the outcome of wars – and that’s a problem
articles.summary: Betting platforms are now accepting wagers on military conflicts and their outcomes, turning wars into gambling opportunities. This crosses a moral red line that could trigger regulatory crackdowns across the entire prediction market industry.
